task_completion.description: Open and read the geo_sat_specs.txt document to get the mission parameters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window displaying the contents of ~/Desktop/geo_sat_specs.txt.

task_completion.description: Open GMAT and configure the GTO-to-GEO transfer mission.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GMAT (General Mission Analysis Tool) GUI is open, showing the creation of spacecraft, propagators, or impulsive burns in the Resources tree.

task_completion.description: Use a DifferentialCorrector to target the required GEO insertion.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Target' sequence in the GMAT Mission tree, or a 'DifferentialCorrector' object (e.g., DefaultDC) visible in the Solvers folder of the Resources tree.

task_completion.description: Save the resulting GMAT script to the specified missions directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, file manager, or terminal showing the file 'geo_transfer.script' located in the '~/Documents/missions/' directory.

task_completion.description: Record the required mission results to geo_transfer_results.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing 'geo_transfer_results.txt' in '~/GMAT_output/' containing values for DeltaV1_mps, DeltaV2_mps, TotalDeltaV_mps, GEO_SMA_km, GEO_ECC, and GEO_INC_deg.

integrity.description: The agent actually ran the simulation in GMAT to obtain the results.
integrity.visual_evidence: The GMAT GUI shows a completed run, such as an orbit track in the graphics window, or output in the message window/report file, rather than the agent just guessing the numbers.

integrity.description: The recorded results match the actual GMAT simulation outputs.
integrity.visual_evidence: The values typed into 'geo_transfer_results.txt' correspond to the values displayed in the GMAT output reports or command window.

integrity.description: The agent saved the files in the exact directories requested, not just on the Desktop.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target GEO Semi-Major Axis (GEO_SMA_km) should be approximately 42164.17 km (acceptable range: 42114 to 42214 km). The expected DeltaV2 (Apogee Kick) is between 1400.0 and 1900.0 m/s. The Total DeltaV should be between 1400.0 and 2000.0 m/s. The task strictly requires GEO_ECC < 0.0005 and GEO_INC_deg < 0.05 degrees, though the underlying verifier may accept up to 0.005 and 0.5 degrees respectively. Ensure the recorded values fall within these physically accurate bounds.
